title: cohniella sprucei
intro: Cohniella sprucei, also known as the "Mazatec's dust", is a species of fungus that grows on the dung of various mammals in Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antifungal activities. The health benefits associated with Cohniella sprucei include its ability to stimulate the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. The fungus is typically prepared as a tincture or decoction by the Mazatec people, who use it in their traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments.
